config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama karena tanpa perlindungan terhadap individu, kepentingan masyarakat gampang berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan, pendapat, dan martabat orang. Di Indonesia sendiri saya melihat betapa pentingnya menjaga keseimbangan: kepentingan bersama memang perlu, tetapi tidak boleh mengorbankan hak dasar warga seenaknya. Masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dari individu yang dilindungi, bukan yang dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have learned how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse abuse of power. Public safety and shared goals matter, but they should be limited by strong individual rights, especially speech, due process, privacy, and religious freedom. When society’s interests override those rights too casually, minorities and unpopular people are usually the first to pay the price. A healthy society protects individuals first and then asks them to contribute to the common good within those limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang seharusnya dijaga adalah keseimbangan antara ketertiban sosial dan martabat orang per orang. Hak individu harus jadi batas yang jelas, karena tanpa itu kelompok kecil dan orang yang tidak populer justru paling mudah dikorbankan. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling kuat menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil tetap mengurus kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a blanket rule, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify overreach and suppress dissent. Public health, safety, and civic order matter, but they are legitimate only when they are constrained by firm rights like due process, free speech, and privacy. The point is not to elevate selfishness; it is to make sure the majority cannot simply steamroll minorities or unpopular people in the name of “the common good.” A just society serves the public by protecting individuals first, then asking them to accept shared responsibilities within those limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu, karena kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh dipakai sebagai alasan untuk mengalahkan hak individu secara otomatis. Di Indonesia, saya melihat frasa “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dijadikan pembenar untuk pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal justru hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, privasi, dan perlakuan adil harus menjadi pagar utama. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat batas yang jelas, bukan dengan membiarkan mayoritas menekan minoritas atau orang yang tidak populer. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling keras menuntut pengorbanan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga ketertiban tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of language has often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. Public safety and the common good matter, but they are only legitimate when they stay within firm limits like due process, free speech, and privacy. I also disagree with the idea that a society becomes stronger by demanding sacrifice first; in my view, it becomes stronger by protecting people from being steamrolled by the majority. The right balance is collective responsibility with individual rights as the boundary, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
